nodes.title: Am Architectural History I
nodes.description: Study of architectural history to gain fluency in the stylistic terms so essential to historic preservation and to public support for conserving our architectural heritage. Prerequisites: Historic Preservation Graduate student; or twelve hours of History, minimum Junior standing; or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Historic Preservation Graduate student; or minimum Junior standing; or Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Master's Thesis Research
nodes.description: Total of six hours required.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Culture, Nature, and Community
nodes.description: Seminar drawing from UVM's important relationships with the National Park Service and using the country's National Parks as places where the merger of cultural and natural resources provides opportunities for meaningful communication about the American experience and current issues in historic preservation. Prerequisite: Historic Preservation Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Historic Preservation Graduate student. HP Graduate students only or instructor permission.

nodes.title: Am Architectural History II
nodes.description: Continuation of HP 5200. Study of architectural history to gain fluency in the stylistic terms so essential to historic preservation and to public support for conserving our architectural heritage. Prerequisite: HP 5200 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. HP Graduate students only or instructor permission; Open to Degree and PACE students;

nodes.title: Graduate Independent Research
nodes.description: Student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Rschg Historic Properties
nodes.description: Methods for researching historic structures and sites using archival and physical evidence, deciphering archaic building technologies, and documenting structures through professional reports, architectural photography, measured drawings. Prerequisite: Historic Preservation Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Historic Preservation Graduate student or instructor permission.

nodes.title: Practicum: Management
nodes.description: Practicum accompanying HP 6308. Co-requisite: HP 6308.
nodes.other_requirements: Co-requisite: HP 6308.

nodes.title: Heritage Studies
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Practicum: Conservation II
nodes.description: Practicum accompanying HP 6307 Architectural Conservation II. Co-requisite: HP 6307.
nodes.other_requirements: 3rd semester HP grad; Students are required to attend two one-day on-campus sessions; Instructor will provide the dates of the on-campus sessions.

nodes.title: Practicum: Arch History I
nodes.description: Practicum accompanying HP 5200 American Architectural History I. Co-requisite: HP 5200.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission; Students must be enrolled concurrently in HP 5200 to register; Students are required to attend two day-long on-campus sessions; Instructor will provide dates of the on-campus sessions. Two practicum sessions will be held, one in mid-September and one in mid-October. Exact dates of the two practicum sessions will be announced at the start of the semester. Practicum sessions supplement the classroom learning and often involve travel off campus to historic sites and projects around the state. In-person attendance of the two, day-long practicum sessions is mandatory.

nodes.title: Practicum: Arch History II
nodes.description: Practicum accompanying HP 5202 American Architectural History II. Co-requisite: HP 5202.
nodes.other_requirements: HP Graduate students only or instructor permission; Students must be enrolled concurrently in HP 5202 to register; Students required to attend two day-long on-campus session; Instructor will provide dates of the on-campus session.

nodes.title: Practicum: ResearchingHistProp
nodes.description: Practicum accompanying HP 5206 Researching Historic Structures and Sites. Co-requisite: HP 5206.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission; Students must be enrolled concurrently in HP 5206 to register; Students are required to attend two day-long on-campus sessions

nodes.title: Mgmt of Historic Site Museums
nodes.description: Provides essential training for the management of historic site museums and will give students an opportunity to study and apply best practices established by national standards for preservation and restoration in the context of Vermont's state historic sites as study models. Prerequisite: HP 6306.
nodes.other_requirements: 3rd semester HP grad or instructor permission

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; 1-6 credits

nodes.title: Practicum: Policy and Planning
nodes.description: Practicum accompanying HP 6304 Historic Preservation Policy and Planning. Co-requisite: HP 6304.
nodes.other_requirements: Students must be enrolled concurrently in HP 6304 to register; Students are required to attend two day-long on-campus sessions; Instructor will provide dates of the on-campus sessions. Two practicum sessions will be held, one in mid-September and one in mid-October. Exact dates of the two practicum sessions will be announced at the start of the semester. Practicum sessions supplement the classroom learning and often involve travel off campus to historic sites and projects around the state. In-person attendance of the two, day-long practicum sessions is mandatory.

nodes.title: Practicum: Conservation I
nodes.description: Practicum accompanying HP 6306 Architectural Conservation I. Co-requisite: HP 6306.
nodes.other_requirements: Co-requisite: HP 6306. Students must be registered concurrently in HP 6306 to register; Students are required to attend two day-long on-campus session; Instructor will provide dates of the on-campus session

nodes.title: HstPres Practice Methods
nodes.description: Introduces students to professional practice methods for conducting historic site and structures surveys. National Register nominations and rehabilitation investment tax credit application projects. Prerequisite: HP 5200.
nodes.other_requirements: HP Graduate students only or instructor permission

nodes.title: Practicum: Culture, Nature
nodes.description: Practicum accompanying HP 6301 Culture, Nature, and Community. Co-requisite: HP 6301.
nodes.other_requirements: Students must be enrolled concurrently in HP 6301 to register; Students are required to attend two day-long on-campus session; Instructor will provide dates of the on-campus session

nodes.title: Practicum: Practice Methods
nodes.description: Practicum accompanying HP 6305 Historic Preservation Practical Methods. Co-requisite: HP 6305.
nodes.other_requirements: Students must be enrolled concurrently in HP 6305 to register; Students are required to attend two day-long on-campus session; Instructor will provide dates of the on-campus session

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: Participants will devote a semester to preservation within an appropriate institution or agency. Prerequisite: HP 6304 or HP 6305.
nodes.other_requirements: HP 2 Grads only

nodes.title: Architectural Conservation II
nodes.description: Further exploration of architectural conservation emphasizing an integrated examination of historic preservation through lectures, seminars, and field and laboratory research projects. Prerequisite: HP 6306.
nodes.other_requirements: 3rd semester HP grad or instructor permission

nodes.title: Intro to Historic Preservation
nodes.description: Introduction to the preservation of historic built and cultural environments, with emphasis on the history of the historic preservation movement in America; laws available to protect historic resources; policy making considerations; national standards for preservation, rehabilitation, restoration, and reconstruction; and best practices for managing historic sites and structures. Prerequisites: A Catamount Core WIL1 course; minimum Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Sophomore standing. Undergraduate students only

nodes.title: HstPres Policy and Planning
nodes.description: Introduction to the professional practice of preservation planning. Traces the evolution of the historic preservation movement and examines contemporary preservation policy-making issues. Prerequisite: HP 5200.
nodes.other_requirements: HP 1 Grads only or instructor permission

nodes.title: Architectural Conservation I
nodes.description: An examination of the physical properties of historic building materials, their deterioration mechanisms, and strategies for assessing conditions, conserving and rehabilitating historic resources. Prerequisite: HP 5206.
nodes.other_requirements: HP majors only or instructor permission
